mapper.sql: e.id, e.employee_no, e.username, e.password_hash, e.real_name, e.department_id, d.name AS department_name, e.position_id, p.name AS position_name, e.leader_id, l.real_name AS leader_name, e.phone, e.email, e.status, e.created_at, e.updated_at
FROM employee e LEFT JOIN department d ON d.id = e.department_id LEFT JOIN position p ON p.id = e.position_id LEFT JOIN employee l ON l.id = e.leader_id
mapper.select: SELECT e.id AS applicant_id, direct_leader.id AS direct_leader_id, direct_leader.real_name AS direct_leader_name, department_leader.id AS department_leader_id, department_leader.real_name AS department_leader_name FROM employee e LEFT JOIN department d ON d.id = e.department_id LEFT JOIN employee direct_leader ON direct_leader.id = e.leader_id AND direct_leader.department_id = e.department_id AND direct_leader.status = 1 AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM employee_role direct_role_link JOIN sys_role direct_role ON direct_role.id = direct_role_link.role_id WHERE direct_role_link.employee_id = direct_leader.id AND direct_role.code = 'SUPER_ADMIN' AND direct_role.status = 1 ) LEFT JOIN employee department_leader ON department_leader.id = d.leader_employee_id AND department_leader.department_id = e.department_id AND department_leader.status = 1 AND NOT EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM employee_role department_role_link JOIN sys_role department_role ON department_role.id = department_role_link.role_id WHERE department_role_link.employee_id = department_leader.id AND department_role.code = 'SUPER_ADMIN' AND department_role.status = 1 ) WHERE e.id = #{applicantId}
mapper.update: UPDATE employee SET real_name = #{realName}, department_id = #{departmentId}, position_id = #{positionId}, leader_id = #{leaderId}, phone = #{phone}, email = #{email}, status = #{status}, updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP WHERE id = #{id}
mapper.select: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee WHERE employee_no = #{employeeNo} AND id != #{excludeId}
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee WHERE username = #{username} AND id != #{excludeId}
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee WHERE status = 1
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee WHERE status != 1
SELECT d.name, COUNT(e.id) AS item_count FROM department d LEFT JOIN employee e ON e.department_id = d.id GROUP BY d.id, d.name ORDER BY item_count DESC, d.id
SELECT p.name, COUNT(e.id) AS item_count FROM position p LEFT JOIN employee e ON e.position_id = p.id GROUP BY p.id, p.name ORDER BY item_count DESC, p.id
